frameworks.name: representational models
frameworks.one_line: Hypotheses about the distribution of activity profiles across experimental conditions

frameworks.name: encoding analysis
frameworks.one_line: Predicts activity patterns from feature models via regression

frameworks.name: pattern component modeling (PCM)
frameworks.one_line: Likelihood-ratio test on the second moment of activity profiles

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis (RSA)
frameworks.one_line: Compares condition-pair dissimilarity structures across models

core_question: How are encoding analysis, PCM, and RSA mathematically related, and how do they compare in power to adjudicate between representational models?
key_assumptions: Representational geometry is captured by the second moment of activity profiles
Activity profiles across conditions follow a definable distribution
Decodability of features depends on representational geometry
manipulations: Simulated data across three different experimental designs
Compared encoding analysis, PCM, and RSA on the same datasets
Varied which competing representational models were tested
key_findings: All three methods evaluate the same second moment of activity profile distributions
PCM provides the most powerful test when its assumptions hold
Encoding analysis and RSA can perform similarly when conducted appropriately
The three methods differ in computational demands, ease of use, and extensibility
interpretations: The three analyses share a common mathematical core via the second moment, making them unifiable under one framework
Each method renders different aspects of representational information explicit and has distinct practical advantages
limitations_noted: PCM's power advantage depends on its assumptions holding
